facets.domain: What the problem is about. Attaches to the problem; refs are pNNNN.
facets.technique: How a solution works. Attaches to a solution index; refs are pNNNN_sN.
facets.takeaway: Transferable principle. Curated (no URL); refs (pNNNN) assigned by hand.

tags.name: 3-smooth number
tags.summary: An integer whose prime factors are all at most 3.

tags.name: Ackermann function
tags.summary: A rapidly growing recursive function that is a classic example of a computable but not primitive recursive function.

tags.name: Algebraic conjugate
tags.summary: Pairing an algebraic number with its conjugate so their powers sum to an integer, forcing the fractional part toward 0 or 1.

tags.name: Angle bisector
tags.summary: A line dividing an angle into two equal parts, used here to construct points cutting a triangle's sides.

tags.name: Angle bisector and tangent line to circumcircle
tags.summary: Geometric configuration involving the angle bisector of a triangle and the tangent line to its circumscribed circle.

tags.name: Angular bisector and tangent to circumcircle
tags.summary: Geometric configuration involving the angle bisector of a triangle and the tangent line to its circumscribed circle.

tags.name: April Fools' Day Request for Comments
Automorphic number
tags.summary: A number whose square ends in the same digits as the number itself, in a given base.

tags.name: Bézier curve
tags.summary: A parametric curve defined by repeated linear interpolation of control points.

tags.name: Bi-unitary divisor
tags.summary: A divisor d of n whose only unitary divisor in common with n/d is 1.

tags.name: Box-Ball System
tags.summary: A discrete integrable dynamical system where balls in boxes move according to a fixed rule, exhibiting soliton-like behavior.

tags.name: Brahmagupta's formula
tags.summary: Gives the maximum possible area of a quadrilateral with given side lengths, achieved when it is cyclic.

tags.name: Chromatic polynomial
tags.summary: A polynomial counting the number of proper colourings of a graph as a function of the number of colours.

tags.name: Circumscribed and inscribed circles
tags.summary: The circumcircle and incircle of a triangle-like figure, and the geometric relationships between their centres and radii.

tags.name: Circumcenter and orthocenter of a triangle
tags.summary: The relationship between a triangle's circumscribed circle center and the intersection point of its altitudes.

tags.name: Circumradius of a triangle
tags.summary: The radius of the unique circle passing through all three vertices of a triangle.

tags.name: Coin fountain
tags.summary: Arrangements of coins stacked in rows where each coin touches exactly two coins in the row below.

tags.name: Combinatorial geometry
tags.summary: Study of geometric arrangements of points, lines, and figures using combinatorial counting methods.

tags.name: Combinatory logic
tags.summary: A formal system of combinators and rewriting rules (like the A, Z, S symbols here) used to express computation without variables.

tags.name: Condorcet paradox
tags.summary: The phenomenon where collective pairwise preferences among voters can be cyclic, preventing a clear group choice even when individual preferences are transitive.

tags.name: Consecutive integers
tags.summary: A run of successive whole numbers, each required to satisfy some shared property.

tags.name: Constraint satisfaction problem
tags.summary: A problem defined by variables subject to constraints that must all be simultaneously satisfied, as in Kakuro.

tags.name: Convex function
tags.summary: A function whose graph lies below the line segment between any two of its points, key to bounding lattice-point counts along it.

tags.name: Convex hull
tags.summary: The subset of points visible from a given vantage point along a skyline forms an upper convex hull of peak coordinates.

tags.name: Convex polygon
tags.summary: A polygon whose interior angles are all less than 180 degrees, relevant to maximizing area given fixed side lengths.

tags.name: Coupon collector's problem
tags.summary: The classic problem of determining the expected number of random draws needed to collect every distinct item in a set.

tags.name: Cribbage
tags.summary: A card game whose scoring rules (pairs, runs, fifteens) define the combinatorial counting problem.

tags.name: Cyclic polygon
tags.summary: A polygon whose vertices lie on a common circle, which maximizes area for a given multiset of side lengths.

tags.name: De Bruijn sequence
tags.summary: A cyclic sequence of symbols in which every possible length-N subsequence appears exactly once.

tags.name: Ellipse
tags.summary: A conic section defined as the locus of points with constant sum of distances to two foci, here rotated and intersected with a copy of itself.

tags.name: English numerals
tags.summary: The conventions for spelling out numbers as English words, including British 'and' usage and scale words like hundred and thousand.

tags.name: Epistemic logic
tags.summary: Reasoning about what agents know, and what they know about others' knowledge, used to model puzzles where players deduce hidden values from mutual ignorance.

tags.name: Equivalence relation
tags.summary: A relation partitioning objects into classes of equivalent items, used here to count non-equivalent tilings.

tags.name: Eulerian path
tags.summary: A closed or open walk in a graph that traverses every edge exactly once.

tags.name: Exponential distribution
tags.summary: A continuous probability distribution modeling the time between events with a constant rate, used here for groundwater depth.

tags.name: Fibonacci word
tags.summary: The golden-ratio Beatty/Fibonacci binary sequence used as the second component combined via XOR to form the problem's string.

tags.name: Floor function
tags.summary: Rounding down to the nearest integer, central to counting lattice points satisfying a floor-based parity condition.

tags.name: Four exponentials conjecture
tags.summary: A number-theoretic conjecture about the transcendence of certain exponential expressions, invoked here to justify treating the logarithm as proto-logarithmic.

tags.name: Fractran
tags.summary: An esoteric Turing-complete programming language based on multiplying a state integer by a list of fractions.

tags.name: Frobenius number (coin problem)
tags.summary: The largest value not representable as a non-negative integer linear combination of a given set of coprime integers.

tags.name: Game theory
tags.summary: Strategic decision-making among rational agents, here modeling pirates voting on treasure distribution.

tags.name: Geometric probability
tags.summary: Probability problems involving randomly chosen points or shapes in geometric regions.

tags.name: Geometric progression
tags.summary: A sequence of numbers where each term after the first is found by multiplying the previous term by a fixed, non-zero ratio.

tags.name: Goldbach's conjecture
tags.summary: A family of conjectures about expressing numbers as sums involving primes, including this problem's disproven 'other' variant.

tags.name: Greedy algorithm simulation
tags.summary: A process where a fixed, suboptimal step-by-step strategy is simulated and compared against optimal behaviour.

tags.name: Hamiltonian cycle
tags.summary: A cycle that visits every vertex of a graph exactly once before returning to its start.

tags.name: Hamiltonian path
tags.summary: A path in a graph that visits every vertex exactly once, the structure underlying the board tours counted here.

tags.name: Harmonic number
tags.summary: The nth harmonic number is the sum of the reciprocals of the first n positive integers.

tags.name: Harmonic series
tags.summary: The divergent series of reciprocals of positive integers, and modified variants like the Kempner series that converge when certain terms are omitted.

tags.name: Heron's method
tags.summary: An iterative Newton-like method for approximating square roots by repeated averaging.

tags.name: Hexagonal lattice
tags.summary: A triangular/hexagonal arrangement of lattice points where each point has six equidistant neighbours.

tags.name: Hypocycloid
tags.summary: A curve traced by a point on a small circle rolling inside a larger circle.

tags.name: Idempotent matrix
tags.summary: A matrix M satisfying M^2 = M.

tags.name: Incircle and excircles of a triangle
Inequality
tags.summary: A relation comparing quantities that are not equal, used to bound sequence terms.

tags.name: Adaptive binary search / decision tree information theory
tags.summary: Finding the minimal number of yes/no questions needed to identify an unknown item among possibilities, a decision-tree search problem.

tags.name: Irrational number
tags.summary: A real number that cannot be written as a ratio of integers and whose decimal expansion never terminates or repeats.

tags.name: Isoperimetric inequality
tags.summary: The relationship bounding the maximum area enclosable for a given perimeter length.

tags.name: Kaprekar's constant
tags.summary: A fixed point reached by repeatedly sorting a number's digits and subtracting the smaller arrangement from the larger.

tags.name: Khinchin's constant
tags.summary: The almost-everywhere limiting geometric mean of continued fraction partial quotients.

tags.name: Kolakoski sequence
tags.summary: A self-describing sequence over two symbols whose own run lengths reproduce the sequence.

tags.name: Lambda calculus
tags.summary: A formal system for expressing computation via function abstraction, application, and variable binding.

tags.name: Langton's ant
tags.summary: A two-state, four-direction Turing-machine-like ant that walks on a grid flipping cell colours according to simple local rules, eventually building a recurring 'highway' pattern.

tags.name: Lenticular hole
tags.summary: A lens-shaped convex region enclosed between two intersecting circles.

tags.name: Lights Out (puzzle)
tags.summary: A toggle-based grid puzzle where selecting a cell flips it and its edge-adjacent neighbors, with solvability analyzed via linear algebra over GF(2).

tags.name: Liouville function
tags.summary: A multiplicative function of a positive integer defined by the parity of the total count of prime factors with multiplicity.

tags.name: Longest increasing subsequence
tags.summary: The problem of finding the longest (weakly or strictly) monotonic subsequence of a sequence, here applied to a number's decimal digits.

tags.name: Look-and-say sequence
tags.summary: A sequence formed by describing consecutive runs of digits in the previous term.

tags.name: Median
tags.summary: The middle value of a sorted list, used here to define sliding-window medians of a generated sequence.

tags.name: Mex (minimum excludant)
tags.summary: The smallest non-negative (or positive) integer not present in a set, used here to define the sequence-generating process.

tags.name: Moving sofa problem
tags.summary: Finding the largest shape that can be maneuvered through an L-shaped corridor.

tags.name: Necklace (combinatorics)
tags.summary: Counting arrangements of colored beads around a circle up to rotation, using Burnside's lemma.

tags.name: Nontransitive dice
tags.summary: Sets of dice where the relation 'beats with probability > 1/2' forms a cycle rather than a total order.

tags.name: Partizan game
tags.summary: A combinatorial game where the moves available to each player differ, unlike impartial games.

tags.name: Perfect cube
tags.summary: An integer that is the third power of another integer, the building block of the cube-subtraction procedure in this problem.

tags.name: Planar graph
tags.summary: A graph that can be drawn without edge crossings, relevant to connecting pairs above or below a line without intersections.

tags.name: Kernel of a polygon
tags.summary: The set of points from which a polygon's entire boundary is visible, used to define visibility/star-shaped properties of polygons.

tags.name: Polyomino
tags.summary: A shape formed by joining identical squares edge-to-edge, the basic object being counted and balanced in this problem.

tags.name: Prime factorization
tags.summary: Decomposing a number into its prime power components to compute multiplicative properties like divisor counts.

tags.name: Prime-proof number
tags.summary: A number for which changing any single decimal digit, other than the leading digit to zero, never produces a prime.

tags.name: Primitive root modulo n
tags.summary: An integer whose powers generate all nonzero residues modulo a prime.

tags.name: Product-sum number
tags.summary: A natural number that can be expressed both as the sum and as the product of the same multiset of natural numbers.

tags.name: Protein folding
tags.summary: Modeling a protein as an HP-string folded on a lattice to maximize favorable H-H contacts.

tags.name: Quadtree
tags.summary: A tree data structure recursively partitioning a 2D region into four quadrants, used here to encode a black-and-white image.

tags.name: Radical of an integer
tags.summary: The product of the distinct prime factors of a positive integer, central to the abc-hit condition rad(abc) < c.

tags.name: Reversible number
tags.summary: A positive integer whose sum with its digit-reversal consists entirely of odd decimal digits.

tags.name: Roulette (curve)
tags.summary: The curve traced by a point attached to a curve as it rolls without slipping along another curve.

tags.name: Rubik's Cube group
tags.summary: The group of permutations of a Rubik's Cube's cubelets/faces reachable by legal moves, used to classify colourings up to equivalence.

tags.name: Rudin-Shapiro sequence
tags.summary: A binary sequence derived from counting adjacent one-bits in the binary expansion of n.

tags.name: Self-intersection
tags.summary: A point where a curve crosses itself, common in the study of parametric curves.

tags.name: Seven-segment display
tags.summary: A display made of segments that light up to represent digits, whose on/off transitions determine energy cost.

tags.name: Simpson's paradox
tags.summary: A statistical phenomenon where a trend appearing in each group of data reverses when the groups are combined.

tags.name: Sliding block puzzle
tags.summary: A puzzle where pieces confined to a grid are moved by sliding to reach different configurations.

tags.name: Spanning tree
tags.summary: A subgraph that connects all vertices of a graph with no cycles, exactly one such path existing between any two vertices.

tags.name: Sqube
tags.summary: A number of the form p^2 q^3 for distinct primes p and q, the object defined in Project Euler Problem 200.

tags.name: Standard Young tableau
tags.summary: A filling of a (skew) shape with numbers increasing along rows and columns, the combinatorial object being counted for the gnomon grid.

tags.name: Star polygon
tags.summary: A self-intersecting polygon {p/q} formed by connecting every q-th vertex of a regular p-gon, wrapping q times around the centre.

tags.name: Steiner chain
tags.summary: A closed cyclic chain of circles each tangent to two fixed circles and to its neighbours in the chain.

tags.name: Steiner inellipse
tags.summary: The unique largest-area ellipse inscribed in a triangle, tangent to the midpoints of its sides.

tags.name: Steinhaus-Johnson-Trotter algorithm
tags.summary: An algorithm that generates all permutations of n elements via single adjacent transpositions, forming a Gray code on permutations.

tags.name: Structural rigidity
tags.summary: The property of a framework of rods and hinges being unable to flex, studied via rigidity theory of embedded graphs.

tags.name: Substring
tags.summary: A contiguous run of characters (here, digits) taken from within a larger string or number.

tags.name: Sudoku
tags.summary: A 9x9 grid puzzle requiring each digit 1-9 exactly once per row, column, and 3x3 box.

tags.name: Thue-Morse sequence
tags.summary: A binary sequence defined by recursively negating or duplicating terms, central to this problem's subsequence condition.

tags.name: Tiling
tags.summary: Covering a region (here a 1D row) completely using a set of tile shapes without overlap.

tags.name: Tower of Hanoi
tags.summary: Classic puzzle of moving a stack of differently-sized disks between rods under size-ordering restrictions.

tags.name: Tribonacci number
tags.summary: A generalization of the Fibonacci sequence in which each term is the sum of the preceding three terms.

tags.name: Trigonometric functions
tags.summary: Functions of angles such as sine and cosine used to define curves and periodic behavior.

tags.name: Lissajous curve
tags.summary: A curve traced by parametric equations combining sinusoidal motions in two perpendicular directions.

tags.name: Turán's theorem
tags.summary: Extremal graph theory result bounding the number of edges a graph can have while avoiding a large independent set, which underlies the fuse-testing strategy in this problem.

tags.name: Unitary divisor
tags.summary: A divisor d of n such that gcd(d, n/d) = 1, i.e. d and n share no repeated prime factor between them.

tags.name: Variance
tags.summary: A measure of the spread of a set of numbers, defined as the average of squared deviations from the mean.

tags.name: Young diagram
tags.summary: A finite collection of left- and top-aligned squares arranged in non-increasing rows and columns, used to model partitions and combinatorial games.

tags.name: Branch and bound
tags.summary: Pruning a search tree by comparing an optimistic bound on each partial solution against the best complete solution found so far.

tags.name: Descartes' theorem
tags.summary: A relation among the curvatures of four mutually tangent circles, used to find the curvature of the circle filling a gap.

tags.name: Divisor-sum sieve
tags.summary: Compute the sum of proper divisors for every n up to N in O(N log N) by adding each candidate divisor onto all of its multiples, generalising the Sieve of Eratosthenes' additive pass.

tags.name: Fibonacci fast doubling
tags.summary: Uses the identities F(2k)=F(k)(2F(k+1)-F(k)) and F(2k+1)=F(k)^2+F(k+1)^2 to compute F(n) mod m in O(log n) multiplications by scanning n's bits.

tags.name: Floor-sum algorithm
tags.summary: Evaluates sum_{i=0}^{n-1} floor((a*i+b)/m) in O(log m) via a Euclidean-style recursive reduction, avoiding per-term iteration.

tags.name: Gauss summation trick
tags.summary: Pairing the ends of an arithmetic run to sum it in closed form.

tags.name: In-place algorithm
tags.summary: Overwriting the input matrix itself as the DP table to avoid allocating a separate structure.

tags.name: Kummer's theorem (application)
tags.summary: Counts base-p carries when adding k and n-k to test a binomial coefficient's squarefreeness without ever factoring the (huge) value.

tags.name: Linear (Euler) sieve
tags.summary: A sieve that visits each composite exactly once via its smallest prime factor, enabling O(n) construction of multiplicative functions.

tags.name: math.comb
tags.summary: Uses Python's standard-library math.comb to compute binomial coefficients directly instead of building a Pascal's-triangle table.

tags.name: Matrix exponentiation
tags.summary: Representing a linear recurrence as repeated multiplication by a fixed transfer matrix, computed via fast exponentiation.

tags.name: Prime factorization (application)
tags.summary: Decomposing a number into its prime power components to compute multiplicative properties like divisor counts.

tags.name: Randomized incremental algorithm
tags.summary: Processing inputs in random order so that each insertion's expected update cost is small, yielding expected-linear-time algorithms like the incremental closest-pair method.

tags.name: Algorithm beats language
tags.summary: A better asymptotic class in a slow language beats a worse one in a fast language.

tags.name: Order checks by cost
tags.summary: Test cheap necessary conditions before expensive ones so failing candidates are pruned before paying for costly checks.

tags.name: Prefer closed form over iteration
tags.summary: Replace an O(n) accumulation with an O(1) formula when the data is structured.

tags.name: Decompose overlapping conditions
tags.summary: Inclusion-exclusion turns one tangled test into independent summable parts.

tags.name: Use exact arithmetic for equality checks
tags.summary: Represent computed geometric or numeric values as exact integers or reduced fractions when the answer depends on testing equality, since floating point can spuriously merge or split truly equal values.

tags.name: Exploit symmetry and complementary counting
tags.summary: Count the complement, or one representative per orbit, when it is smaller.

tags.name: Hold complexity fixed across ports
tags.summary: Keep the C and Python algorithms identical so benchmarks compare constant factors.

tags.name: Precompute once, reuse
tags.summary: Amortise an expensive table across many queries when inputs share structure.

tags.name: Reduce algebraically before coding
tags.summary: Simplify the math first; the cheapest loop is the one you eliminate.

tags.name: Match the abstraction to the hot path
tags.summary: Generators/laziness win on memory, not on a tight numeric loop.

tags.name: Size the work to the input
tags.summary: Bound sieves, tables and caches by the actual inputs, built inside solve().

tags.name: Watch integer width
tags.summary: Anticipate 32/64-bit overflow in C where Python bignums silently cope.
